alignments.rationale: Both claims state WD-DTL effectively handles distribution differences; the publication emphasizes the outcome (addressing distributional differences) without restating the adversarial-training mechanism, so the claim is clarified in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of WD-DTL is verified through 3 transfer scenarios and 16 transfer fault diagnosis experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: WD-DTL achieves the highest diagnostic accuracies compared to existing Maximum Mean Discrepancy and CNN networks in almost all transfer tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the experimental verification but emphasizes comparative superiority (highest accuracies in almost all tasks), which strengthens the result relative to the preprint's statement about experiments.

alignments.pub_claim_text: WD-DTL performs both supervised and unsupervised learning for fault diagnosis tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an operational detail (support for supervised and unsupervised learning) that is not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method predicts users' gender and age based on their behavior, services, and contract information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method predicts users’ gender and age based on their behavior, services, and contract information.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with essentially identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieved 85.6% accuracy in user gender prediction and 65.5% accuracy in user age prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieved 85.6% accuracy in user gender prediction and 65.5% accuracy in user age prediction.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance results are reproduced exactly in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work improves the accuracy of predicting user demographic attributes based on mobile phone data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work improves the accuracy of predicting user demographic attributes based on mobile phone data.
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication lists this conclusion with reduced certainty (medium), so confidence is marked as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model utilizes call detail records, customer relationship management, and billing information as data sources.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims provided do not explicitly restate the specific data-source listing from the preprint; thus this specific preprint detail is absent from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide a unified learning-theoretic methodology for modeling economic solution problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide a unified learning-theoretic methodology for modeling economic solutions from data.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same high-level contribution of a unified learning-theoretic methodology; wording differences are minor and the claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our learning theoretic framework generalizes the graph dimension to the solution concept learning domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our learning-theoretic framework generalizes the notion of function space dimension to the solution concept learning domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the technical object being generalized (function space dimension vs graph dimension), clarifying the formal notion while preserving the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identify sufficient conditions for the PAC learnability of solution concepts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identify sufficient conditions for efficient solution learnability.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from PAC learnability broadly to 'efficient solution learnability,' changing the claim's focus and reducing stated certainty, hence a revision and narrowing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Results in existing works can be immediately derived using our methodology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Results in existing works can be immediately derived using our methodology.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this claim verbatim, preserving its scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We yield a novel notion of PAC competitive equilibrium and PAC Condorcet winners in other economic domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We yield learning variants of competitive equilibria and Condorcet winners in other economic domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim as 'learning variants' rather than explicitly labeling them PAC variants; the result is preserved but worded differently with lower stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed convolutional sparse coding (CSC) methods maintain more details and avoid artifacts compared to traditional dictionary learning (DL)-based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods maintain more details and avoid artifacts caused by patch aggregation in sparse-view CT reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance advantage (fewer artifacts and preserved details) and specifies the artifact source (patch aggregation), so the claim content is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methods do not require dividing the image into overlapped patches, which is a limitation of traditional DL-based computed tomography (CT) reconstruction methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional sparse coding (CSC) addresses the limitations of traditional dictionary learning (DL)-based computed tomography (CT) reconstruction methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the preprint's more specific procedural claim as a general conclusion that CSC addresses DL-based method limitations, with slightly reduced certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments with simulated and real CT data validate the effectiveness of the proposed methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that the proposed methods achieve better performance than several existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert experimental validation and improved performance; the publication explicitly states qualitative and quantitative superiority, matching the preprint's validation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that the proposed methods achieve better performance than several existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The qualitative and quantitative results demonstrate that the proposed methods achieve better performance than several existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: This claim is reproduced directly in the publication, preserving content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed data-driven geometry compression method outperforms the MPEG reference solution in terms of rate-distortion with 51.5% BDBR savings on average.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-driven geometry compression method outperforms the MPEG reference solution in terms of rate-distortion on the Microsoft Voxelized Upper Bodies dataset with 51.5% BDBR savings on average.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the numeric BDBR savings but specifies the dataset used, narrowing the claim's scope to that dataset while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method maintains high resolution outputs even at low bitrates, unlike octree-based methods which face exponential diminution of the number of points.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method produces high resolution outputs even at low bitrates, unlike octree-based methods which face exponential diminution of the number of points at low bitrates.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this performance advantage with essentially the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decoding process is effectively cast as a binary classification of the point cloud occupancy map.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decoding process is cast as a binary classification of the point cloud occupancy map.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the same methodological formulation for decoding, with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Multi-Class Graph Cut (MCGC) approach effectively segments individual tree crowns from LiDAR point clouds using local three-dimensional geometry and density information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multiclass graph cut (MCGC) approach effectively segments individual tree crowns from airborne light detection and ranging point clouds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claims are equivalent in scope and wording aside from minor phrasing (LiDAR vs airborne LiDAR); meaning and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our algorithm produces robust estimates of hectare-scale carbon density when compared to estimates from permanent inventory plots. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our algorithm can produce robust estimates of hectare-scale carbon density by measuring individual tree biomass. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result but clarifies the mechanism (via individual tree biomass); the claim's substance and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MCGC approach can robustly identify trees in the top and intermediate layers of the canopy, but it cannot recognize small trees. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MCGC approach robustly identifies trees in the top and intermediate layers of the canopy but cannot recognize small trees. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is reiterated verbatim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexibility of the MCGC method allows for the addition of dimensions of information, such as spectral reflectance, for future development. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The flexibility of the MCGC method to incorporate additional dimensions of information suggests potential for future development. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contribution but frames it more tentatively (lower certainty), so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study demonstrates automated detection and segmentation of brain metastases on multi-sequence MRI using a deep learning approach based on a fully convolution neural network (CNN). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep-learning approach using multisequence MRI can automatically detect and segment brain metastases with high accuracy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central result/claim with equivalent meaning and high confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The area under the ROC-curve (AUC), averaged across all patients, was 0.98. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The area under the ROC curve (AUC), averaged across all patients, was 0.98 ± 0.04. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published paper reports the same central AUC while adding an uncertainty estimate (±0.04), so the claim is clarified but not substantively changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using an average optimal probability threshold determined by the development set, precision, recall, and Dice-score were 0.79, 0.53, and 0.79, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using an average optimal probability threshold determined by the development set, precision, recall, and Dice score were 0.79 ± 0.20, 0.53 ± 0.22, and 0.79 ± 0.12, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical results are the same but the publication provides variability (± values), clarifying the precision of the estimates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: At the same probability threshold, the network showed an average false positive rate of 8.3/patient (no lesion-size limit) and 3.4/patient (10 mm3 lesion size limit). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published article does not report the false-positive-per-patient rates present in the preprint, so this specific quantitative claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed compression method can reduce the bitrate by 25% according to objective evaluation and by 17% according to subjective evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed compression method can produce videos with the same visual quality as regular x264 while reducing the bitrate by 25% according to objective evaluation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the preprint's combined objective/subjective statement and emphasizes the objective 25% reduction with a note on equivalent visual quality; the subjective 17% reduction appears separately in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis demonstrates that saliency models can compete with gaze maps for a single observer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Saliency models can compete with gaze maps for a single observer in terms of video compression quality. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion about saliency models versus gaze maps with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can improve video compression quality without requiring a switch to a new video encoding standard. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can extend to most video bitstream formats and improve video compression quality without requiring a switch to a new video encoding standard. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by asserting applicability to most bitstream formats and reports higher confidence, so scope is broadened and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LSTMs are particularly well-suited for rainfall-runoff forecasting due to their ability to represent dynamic reservoirs and storages. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LSTMs are particularly well-suited for rainfall-runoff forecasting due to their ability to represent dynamic reservoirs and storages. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the conclusion verbatim with the same framing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained LSTM model can be analyzed and interpreted effectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained LSTM model can be analyzed and interpreted effectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is carried over unchanged into the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network internally learns to represent patterns that align with the qualitative understanding of the hydrological system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network learns to represent patterns that align with the qualitative understanding of the hydrological system. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication reports the finding with lower certainty, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most of the unsupervised network representation learning approaches either explicitly or implicitly model and exploit context information of a node. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most unsupervised network representation learning (UNRL) approaches either model and exploit neighborhood or context information of a node. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with synonymous phrasing (neighborhood/context) and unchanged confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is no single method that is a clear winner among the unsupervised network representation learning techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is no single method that is a clear winner for non-attributed graphs in UNRL. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claim narrows the original to non-attributed graphs specifically, so the scope is narrowed though the overall conclusion is similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The choice of a suitable method for unsupervised network representation learning is dictated by certain properties of the embedding methods, task, and structural properties of the underlying graph. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The choice of a suitable UNRL method is dictated by certain properties of the embedding methods, task, and structural properties of the underlying graph. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same recommendation and uncertainty; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper reports common pitfalls in the evaluation of unsupervised network representation learning methods and provides suggestions for experimental design and interpretation of results. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Common pitfalls in the evaluation of UNRL methods have been identified, along with suggestions for experimental design and interpretation of results. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same but is presented with reduced certainty in the publication, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural network-based reinforcement learning can identify thermodynamic trajectories of maximal efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural-network-based reinforcement learning can identify thermodynamic trajectories of maximal efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about neural-network RL finding maximal-efficiency trajectories with the same certainty and type.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gradient-based reinforcement learning is able to learn the Stirling cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gradient-based reinforcement learning is able to learn the Stirling cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about gradient-based RL learning the Stirling cycle is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An evolutionary approach achieves the optimal Carnot cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An evolutionary approach achieves the optimal Carnot cycle. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the evolutionary approach achieving the optimal Carnot cycle with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolutionary scheme learns a previously unknown thermodynamic cycle when given an additional irreversible process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolutionary scheme learns a previously unknown thermodynamic cycle when given an additional irreversible process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The specific result about discovering a previously unknown cycle under added irreversibility is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reinforcement learning strategies developed for game playing can be applied to solve physical problems conditioned upon path-extensive order parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reinforcement learning strategies developed for game playing can be applied to solve physical problems conditioned upon path-extensive order parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim about applying game-playing RL strategies to physics problems is repeated with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed segmentation-based deep-learning architecture outperforms related methods in the specific domain of surface-crack detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed segmentation-based deep-learning architecture outperforms related methods in the specific domain of surface-crack detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is carried over verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture enables training using a small number of samples, specifically approximately 25-30 defective training samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture can be trained using only approximately 25–30 defective training samples, making it practical for industrial applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 25–30 sample claim but explicitly links it to practical industrial applicability, thus clarifying and broadening the original training-size claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep-learning method is practical for industrial use where the number of available defective samples is limited. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture can be trained using only approximately 25–30 defective training samples, making it practical for industrial applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication folds the practicality conclusion into Q1 and states the practicality alongside the training-sample result; the content is preserved but presented with stronger framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset created for the experiments is publicly available to encourage the development and evaluation of new methods for surface-defect detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset created for this research is publicly available to encourage the development and evaluation of new methods for surface-defect detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The availability claim is unchanged in content, but the publication reports a lower certainty level for this contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The movement writing for sleight-of-hand moves with cards taxonomizes all card sleights that have appeared in over 1500 publications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The movement writing for sleight-of-hand moves with cards contains 440 categories of motion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the broad 'taxonomizes all sleights across 1500 publications' claim with a specific enumeration (440 categories), narrowing and revising the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Ontology of Card Sleights is implemented in OWL DL, a Description Logic fragment of the Web Ontology Language. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Ontology of Card Sleights is a computational ontology that extends the Basic Formal Ontology and the Information Artifact Ontology. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both discuss the ontology, but the publication emphasizes its grounding in existing ontologies (BFO/IAO) rather than the implementation language; the focus is clarified/shifted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design criteria used for the ontology ensure it can be accessed and modified with a simple click-and-drag interface. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The design criteria used ensure the ontology can be accessed and modified with a simple click-and-drag interface. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The design-criteria claim about a click-and-drag interface is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This ontology may allow database searches and performance transcriptions by users with card magic knowledge, but no ontology background. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ontology allows for transcription of a performer's actions step by step due to the algorithmic nature of card tricks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses specifically on step-by-step transcription enabled by algorithmic trick structure, narrowing the broader usability/database-search claim made in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the learning process of a neural network on a representative dataset is similar to the accuracy on the original dataset when the neural network architecture is a perceptron and the loss function is the mean squared error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of a perceptron evaluated on the original dataset coincides with the accuracy of the neural network evaluated on the representative dataset under specific conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same result but frames it as valid under explicit 'specific conditions', which clarifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Representativeness of a dataset is measured using persistence diagrams due to its computational efficiency. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Representativeness of a dataset is measured using persistence diagrams due to their computational efficiency. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same methodological claim about using persistence diagrams for representativeness and cites computational efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest that reducing the size of the dataset can gain time in the training process of any neural network. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical results and experimentation suggest that reducing the size of the dataset can decrease the training time of any neural network. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same tentative conclusion (low certainty) that dataset reduction can reduce training time, adding mention of theoretical and experimental support but not altering the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prototype chip of the 2nd generation SpiNNaker system enables the efficient execution of brain-inspired algorithms using low-power ARM processors equipped with a random number generator and an exponential function accelerator. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prototype chip of the second generation SpiNNaker system is designed to efficiently execute brain-inspired algorithms using low-power ARM processors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core design claim but omits the specific mention of RNG and exponential-function accelerators, revising technical detail while preserving the main contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computation time of one plasticity update is reduced by a factor of 2 through efficient use of hardware accelerators and numerical optimizations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computation time of one plasticity update is reduced by a factor of 2 through the use of hardware accelerators and numerical optimizations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantitative result (2x reduction) and its cause are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of the reward-based synaptic sampling model on the SpiNNaker system is the most complex synapse model implemented to date. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the comparative claim that this is the most complex synapse model implemented to date, so this preprint claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model implementation leads to a 62% energy reduction compared to the case without accelerators and the use of external DRAM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of the model leads to a 62% energy reduction compared to the case without accelerators and the use of external DRAM. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The energy reduction figure and comparison are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hardware-software system presented in this work facilitates power-efficient mobile and biomedical applications with biologically plausible brain-inspired algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model implementation is integrated into the SpiNNaker software framework, allowing for scalability onto larger systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the concluding emphasis from enabling mobile/biomedical applications to integration and scalability onto larger systems; the conclusion is thus revised and broadened in system-level scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Data-driven models can define model structures when a priori information is insufficient. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Data-driven models can study model structure when a priori information is insufficient. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with slightly different wording ('define' vs 'study') but preserves the original meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolutionary approach to PDE discovery has fewer restrictions on the PDE form compared to sparse regression methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolutionary approach to PDE discovery has fewer restrictions on the PDE form compared to sparse regression methods. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolutionary method of PDE discovery (EPDE) has been tested on several canonical PDEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolutionary method of PDE discovery (EPDE) has been tested on several canonical PDEs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of the EPDE method is examined using a noised data example. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robustness of the EPDE method is examined using a noised data example. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The robustness test on noisy data is stated the same way with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The metadata about scientific experiments published in online repositories suffer from a high degree of representational heterogeneity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metadata about scientific experiments published in online repositories suffer from a high degree of representational heterogeneity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about representational heterogeneity is identical in content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Normalizing the multiple, distinct field names used in metadata is a crucial step toward uniformly-represented metadata. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Normalizing the multiple, distinct field names used in metadata is a crucial step toward uniformly-represented metadata. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method presented for aligning metadata field names with ontology terms yields more and substantially better alignments compared to the NCBO Annotator. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method based on clustering and embeddings yields more and substantially better alignments between metadata and ontology terms compared to the NCBO Annotator. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides high-quality restoration and segmentation results. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is up to six times faster than an existing Hamiltonian Monte Carlo method. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: An accelerated Markov chain Monte Carlo scheme is adopted using a hierarchical Bayesian model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ECG alleviates the well-known resolution limit issue. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: ECG leads to better stability of the partitions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A detailed understanding of wind turbine performance status classification can improve operations and maintenance in the wind energy industry. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning (DL) algorithms have sparked renewed interest in intelligent machine health monitoring in both industry and academia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent developments in deep learning algorithms have sparked renewed interest in intelligent machine health monitoring.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with minor wording changes but identical meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DL based methods demonstrate superiority over conventional machine learning (ML) methods in terms of fault feature extraction and classification performances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning methods demonstrate superior fault feature extraction and classification performances compared to conventional machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The claim about DL superiority is carried verbatim into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A comparative study shows varying classification accuracy of different algorithms utilizing the Case Western Reserve University (CWRU) bearing dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A comparative study shows varying classification accuracy of different algorithms using the Case Western Reserve University bearing dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result with only minor wording adjustments; the claim content and scope remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides detailed recommendations for applying various DL algorithms to bearing fault diagnostics based on specific application conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides detailed recommendations for applying various deep learning algorithms to bearing fault diagnostics.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the qualifier "based on specific application conditions," removing a constraint and thus broadening the recommendation; the core contribution remains but with a less specific scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method using self-attention networks captures strong geometric changes and generates details using cues from all feature locations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method using self-attention networks captures strong geometric changes and generates details using cues from all feature locations.
alignments.rationale: Claim is identical in the publication and unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of self-attention networks in unsupervised image-to-image translation tasks has not been previously verified.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of self-attention networks in unsupervised image-to-image translation tasks has not been previously verified.
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same, but the publication marks it with higher certainty (medium→high), indicating amplified confidence in the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method compared to existing state-of-the-art unsupervised image-to-image translation methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method compared to existing state-of-the-art unsupervised image-to-image translation methods.
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is reproduced verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning techniques are essential for addressing key problems in IoT wireless communications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning techniques provide a general framework to solve complex problems in IoT wireless communications.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the role of ML from being "essential" to providing a general framework; this is a rephrasing that clarifies the nature of the contribution rather than changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional optimization techniques may not be suitable for IoT devices due to their computational requirements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional optimization techniques may not be suitable for IoT devices due to their energy constraints and the lack of accurate environmental models.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the rationale for unsuitability (adding energy constraints and modeling issues), clarifying and broadening the explanation while keeping the same limitation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The majority of IoT will consist of tiny, mobile, and energy-constrained devices.
alignments.rationale: This demographic/forecast-style claim from the preprint does not appear in the publication; instead the publication emphasizes different contextual aspects (e.g., ad hoc networking).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work provides a comprehensive survey of the application of machine learning in IoT wireless communications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work provides a comprehensive survey of the state of the art in machine learning applications for IoT wireless communications.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the paper as a comprehensive survey of ML applications in IoT wireless communications, with the publication adding "state of the art" as a minor wording enhancement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology allows for quick predictions of tactical solutions to operational problems under imperfect information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology allows for quick predictions of expected tactical descriptions of operational solutions in two-stage stochastic programming.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the theoretical setting (two-stage stochastic programming) and phrases the output as "expected tactical descriptions," narrowing and clarifying the original broader statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms produce highly accurate predictions in very short computing time, specifically in milliseconds or less.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning models produce accurate predictions in very short computing time, specifically in milliseconds or less.
alignments.rationale: The substance is the same (fast, accurate predictions); wording shifts (algorithms→models, highly accurate→accurate) are minor and do not materially change meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction accuracy of the proposed methodology is comparable to solutions computed by sample average approximation of the stochastic program.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictive accuracy of the proposed method is close to the lower bounds calculated based on sample average approximation of the stochastic prediction programs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the comparison more precisely in terms of closeness to lower bounds from SAA rather than direct comparability to SAA solutions, which is a substantive refinement of the accuracy claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seq2seq-based systems have demonstrated a great potential for NLG in the challenge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seq2seq-based systems have demonstrated a great potential for NLG in the challenge.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim with no change in meaning or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seq2seq systems generally score high in terms of word-overlap metrics and human evaluations of naturalness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seq2seq systems generally score high in terms of word-overlap metrics and human evaluations of naturalness.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vanilla seq2seq models often fail to correctly express a given meaning representation if they lack a strong semantic control mechanism applied during decoding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vanilla seq2seq models often fail to correctly express a given meaning representation if they lack a strong semantic control mechanism applied during decoding.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Seq2seq models can be outperformed by hand-engineered systems in terms of overall quality, as well as complexity, length and diversity of outputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Seq2seq models can be outperformed by hand-engineered systems in terms of overall quality, as well as complexity, length and diversity of outputs.
alignments.rationale: The comparative limitation is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research has influenced, inspired and motivated a number of recent studies outwith the original competition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research has influenced, inspired and motivated a number of recent studies outwith the original competition.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Theoretical analyses of evolution strategies are essential for understanding their inner workings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Theoretical analyses of evolution strategies are essential for understanding their inner workings. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with identical wording and certainty, so there is no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-linear difference equations are derived based on one-generation progress rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nonlinear difference equations are derived based on one-generation progress rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is the same in the publication (minor orthographic change only), so classification is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical derivations can predict the dynamics and steady state values of real algorithm runs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical derivations can predict the dynamics and the steady state values of real algorithm runs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same predictive claim with equivalent phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The query complexity of the uniform node sampling scheme for Message Passing Neural Networks is completely independent of the number of the nodes, edges, and neighbors of the input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The query complexity of the uniform node sampling scheme for Message Passing Neural Networks is independent of the number of nodes, edges, and neighbors of the input. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result (minor wording differences only) with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The node sampling method depends only on the error tolerance and confidence probability while providing a theoretical guarantee for the approximation error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The node sampling method depends only on the error tolerance and confidence probability while providing a theoretical guarantee for the approximation error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about dependence on error tolerance and confidence probability and the theoretical guarantee is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first paper to provide a theoretical guarantee of approximation for GNNs within constant time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study is the first to provide a theoretical guarantee of approximation for GNNs within constant time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim (reworded slightly) asserting novelty and constant-time guarantee.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments with synthetic and real-world datasets validated the theoretical results of the node sampling scheme. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments with synthetic and real-world datasets validated the theoretical results regarding the speed and precision of the node sampling scheme. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication matches the experimental validation claim but specifies the validated aspects as speed and precision, which clarifies and narrows the original, more general validation statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed importance sampling estimator effectively couples the reduced-order model and the generative model for uncertainty quantification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed importance sampling estimator effectively couples the reduced-order model and the generative model for uncertainty quantification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Monte Carlo estimator derived from the reduced-order model is biased due to errors from dimension reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Monte Carlo estimator derived from the reduced-order model is biased due to errors from dimension reduction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about bias from dimension reduction is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a weighted empirical distribution improves the processing of data from the reduced-order model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a weighted empirical distribution improves the processing of data from the reduced-order model. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same methodological contribution with the same expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A penalty term in the objective function enhances the robustness of the generative model against overfitting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A penalty term in the objective function enhances the robustness of the generative model against overfitting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results validate the effectiveness of the proposed methodology for estimating the probability of the quantity of interest exceeding a threshold. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed QA approach based on Recognizing Question Entailment (RQE) demonstrates a 29.8% increase over the best official score in the medical task. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICLabel project includes a dataset with spatiotemporal measures for over 200,000 independent components from more than 6000 EEG recordings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: These claims state the same sufficient/ensuring condition for TV minimization to recover piecewise-constant signals (clusters), with matching content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learning accuracy of the proposed algorithm is linked to the set of network flows between data points having known labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The condition for accurate learning depends on the existence of sufficiently large network flows between labeled data points.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe the dependence of accurate learning on the presence/size of network flows between labeled examples; the publication echoes the same connection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness and scalability of the approach is verified by numerical experiments.
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses on theoretical equivalences and conditions and does not state the experimental verification claim present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: TV minimization is equivalent to a minimum cost flow problem through the use of Fenchel duality.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a formal theoretical equivalence (TV minimization <-> min-cost flow) that was not explicitly stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The squeezed model (SVDCNN) is between 10x and 20x smaller than the original VDCNN model, depending on the network depth.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed squeezed model (SVDCNN) is between 10x and 20x smaller than the original Very Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (VDCNN) model, depending on the network depth.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this quantitative size reduction claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The maximum size of the SVDCNN model is maintained at 6MB.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVDCNN model maintains a maximum disk size of 6MB while experiencing a loss in accuracy between 0.4% and 1.3%.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 6MB maximum size claim but combines it with the accuracy-loss information, broadening the claim's content by adding performance trade-off details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVDCNN model experiences an accuracy loss between 0.4% and 1.3% compared to the baseline model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVDCNN model maintains a maximum disk size of 6MB while experiencing a loss in accuracy between 0.4% and 1.3%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative accuracy loss and groups it with the storage-size claim; the accuracy claim itself is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SVDCNN model achieves lower latencies compared to the baseline model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SVDCNN model achieves lower latency over non-dedicated hardware and a higher inference time ratio compared to the baseline model.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the hardware context (non-dedicated) for the latency improvement and adds a related inference-time metric, narrowing and clarifying the original claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Temporal Depthwise Separable Convolutions and Global Average Pooling impacts the network parameters, storage size, dedicated hardware dependence, and accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit methodological/conceptual claim about which architectural choices affect parameters, storage, hardware dependence and accuracy that is not stated in the preprint claims list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computer vision has evolved as a key technology for numerous applications, replacing human supervision.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broad claim about replacing human supervision, instead focusing on specific surveillance/traffic contexts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a survey of relevant visual surveillance research focused on anomaly detection in public places, particularly on roads.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study investigates recent visual surveillance-related research on anomaly detection in public places, particularly on road.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the survey's scope and focus on road/public-place anomaly detection directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey emphasizes learning methods applied to video scenes as the underlying building block of typical anomaly detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey provides a detailed insight into classification techniques, learning methods, datasets, and application contexts related to anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: Both emphasize learning methods in video-anomaly detection; the publication elaborates but keeps the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper summarizes important contributions made in the last six years regarding features, techniques, scenarios, and types of anomalies using a single static camera.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey provides a detailed insight into classification techniques, learning methods, datasets, and application contexts related to anomaly detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the summary-of-contributions role but omits the specific 'last six years' and 'single static camera' constraints, making the published phrasing broader and slightly rephrased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses challenges in computer vision related anomaly detection techniques and outlines important future possibilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study reveals vital gaps in the available datasets and anomaly detection capability in various contexts.
alignments.rationale: The publication turns the general discussion of challenges/future work into an explicit result identifying dataset gaps and capability limitations, a clarification of the preprint's more general statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Timely detection of traffic violations and abnormal behavior of pedestrians through computer vision can be highly effective for maintaining traffic order in cities.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific applied-conclusion about traffic-order maintenance via computer vision that is not explicitly claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Novel approaches are proposed to train Deep Neural Networks for morphing detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approaches for training Deep Neural Networks improve morphing attack detection. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's proposal but clarifies that the approaches lead to improved morphing detection, making the contribution more explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generation of simulated printed-scanned images and other data augmentation strategies improved the performance of morphing detection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Generating simulated printed-scanned images and employing data augmentation strategies contribute to achieving state-of-the-art accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The published paper does not restate the specific claim about pre-training on large face recognition datasets; that element appears omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The regularization scheme introduced improves the focus on phonetic content and achieves performance comparable to top entries in the ZeroSpeech 2017 task. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The discrete encodings extracted using the VQ-VAE facilitate easier mapping to phonemes. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BioBERT largely outperforms BERT and previous state-of-the-art models in a variety of biomedical text mining tasks when pre-trained on biomedical corpora. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BioBERT achieves a 0.62% F1 score improvement in biomedical named entity recognition compared to previous models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BioBERT achieves a 0.62% F1 score improvement in biomedical named entity recognition, a 2.80% F1 score improvement in biomedical relation extraction, and a 12.24% MRR improvement in biomedical question answering. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WiseMove is a software framework designed to investigate safe deep reinforcement learning for motion planning in autonomous driving.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core contribution (a platform/framework for investigating safe DRL for motion planning) with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: WiseMove adopts a modular learning architecture that can be adapted to new technologies and research questions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The platform adopts a modular architecture that reflects the autonomous vehicle software stack.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the modularity claim more specific by linking it to the AV software stack rather than the broader adaptability statement in the preprint, narrowing the claimed scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of WiseMove on a common traffic scenario demonstrates its applicability in ongoing safe learning research.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The initial investigation quantifies the safety and scalability of a state-of-the-art deep reinforcement learning approach in simulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the demonstration-style claim into a more measured, quantitative investigation of safety and scalability in simulation, narrowing applicability and tempering the original demonstration claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of the DRL approach aims to assess its potential use on the vehicle.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains an additional forward-looking evaluation aim about potential vehicle use that does not appear in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm provides a computationally effective, on-line iterative method for estimating the mode of a unimodal smooth density using only samples generated from the density.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed online iterative algorithm effectively estimates the mode of a unimodal smooth density using only samples generated from the density.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the central contribution of the online iterative mode-estimation algorithm without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The asymptotic convergence of the proposed algorithm is established through an ordinary differential equation (ODE) based analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The asymptotic convergence of the proposed algorithm is established through an ordinary differential equation (ODE)-based analysis.
alignments.rationale: The ODE-based convergence analysis claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stability of the estimates from the proposed algorithm is proven by utilizing the concept of regularization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stability of the estimates from the proposed algorithm is proven by utilizing the concept of regularization.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the stability proof claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm for mode estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the experimental claim slightly more specific by naming the task (mode estimation), clarifying but not altering the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial Neural Networks are an efficient representation of highly-entangled many-body quantum states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial neural networks are an efficient representation of highly entangled many-body quantum states. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion in effectively identical language and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of Markov-Chain Monte-Carlo sampling in neural-network states limits their expressive capacity. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of Markov-chain Monte Carlo sampling in neural-network states limits their expressive capacity due to intrinsic computational costs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the limitation claim but adds a causal clarification (intrinsic computational costs), so the content is clarified without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed specialized Neural Network architecture supports efficient and exact sampling, eliminating the need for Markov Chain sampling. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A specialized neural-network architecture is proposed that supports efficient and exact sampling, eliminating the need for Markov-chain sampling. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim with the same scope and emphasis, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach allows for accurate results on larger system sizes than currently accessible to neural-network quantum states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates the ability to obtain accurate results on larger system sizes than those currently accessible to neural-network quantum states. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim in the publication, preserving meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The difficulty in generating precise and uncorrelated samples in MCMC can lead to estimations of observables that are far from their true value. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation—about biased/poor observable estimates from MCMC sampling—is not present in the preprint and thus appears as an added, more specific concern in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical U-Net architecture lacks certain aspects despite its outstanding overall performance in segmenting multimodal medical images. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical U-Net architecture lacks certain aspects despite its outstanding overall performance in segmenting multimodal medical images. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The published text retains the same limitation but presents it with stronger certainty, so the semantic content and scope are unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MultiResUNet is proposed as a potential successor to the U-Net architecture with modifications aimed at improving performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MultiResUNet demonstrates a remarkable gain in performance for challenging images compared to the classical U-Net. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MultiResUNet demonstrates remarkable gains in performance on challenging datasets compared to the classical U-Net. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of MultiResUNet on five different datasets resulted in relative improvements in performance of 10.15%, 5.07%, 2.63%, 1.41%, and 0.62% respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: HBA-KM achieves substantially better competitive ratio and lower variance of return compared to baseline methods, including a Q-learning agent and a UCB-based online learning agent. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Ranking can be used as a proxy task for some regression problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ranking can be used as a proxy task for some regression problems.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks trained to regress to the ground truth targets for labeled data and to simultaneously learn to rank unlabeled data obtain significantly better, state-of-the-art results for both Image Quality Assessment and crowd counting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks trained to regress to the ground truth targets for labeled data and to simultaneously learn to rank unlabeled data obtain significantly better, state-of-the-art results for both Image Quality Assessment and crowd counting.
alignments.rationale: The reported empirical result is unchanged between versions and retains the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Measuring network uncertainty on the self-supervised proxy task is a good measure of informativeness of unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Measuring network uncertainty on the self-supervised proxy task is a good measure of informativeness of unlabeled data.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about uncertainty as an informativeness measure is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the proposed method can reduce labeling effort by up to 50%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the measure of informativeness of unlabeled data can drive an algorithm for active learning, reducing labeling effort by up to 50 percent.
alignments.rationale: The publication links the 50% reduction specifically to using the informativeness measure to drive an active learning algorithm, which clarifies and narrows the original broader claim about the "proposed method."

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm improves feasibility classification of parameter spaces in complex simulation environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm improves feasibility classification in complex simulation environments by exploring unknown parameter spaces iteratively.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the iterative exploration mechanism as part of how the improvement is achieved, clarifying the original claim without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm includes an optimization target that guides exploration towards regions of interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm includes an additional optimization target to guide exploration towards regions of interest.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same contribution (an added optimization target guiding exploration) with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel method demonstrates practical usability in a realistic chemical process simulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel method demonstrates practical usability in a realistic chemical process simulation, achieving a good approximation of the data space topology from relatively few data points.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the demonstration claim but adds performance detail about approximating topology from few points, clarifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm shows advantages over a Kriging-based exploration approach in binary feasibility classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm shows advantages over a Kriging-based exploration approach in a binary feasibility classification scenario.
alignments.rationale: The claim about advantages over a Kriging-based approach is retained with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Vossian Antonomasia is a prolific stylistic device that compresses the introduction or description of a person into a terse formulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Vossian antonomasia is a prolific stylistic device that compresses the introduction or description of a person into a terse formulation.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of Vossian antonomasia is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The phenomenon of Vossian Antonomasia has not been explored using a corpus-based approach until now.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phenomenon of Vossian antonomasia has not been explored using a corpus-based approach until now.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim this is the first corpus-based exploration; wording is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of a full-text newspaper corpus provides new insights into the occurrence and distribution of popular paragons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis of a full-text newspaper corpus provides new insights into the occurrence and distribution of popular paragons.
alignments.rationale: The reported result about insights from corpus analysis is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides new insights into the development of the drosophila wing disc tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides a possible novel indicator for the development of the drosophila wing disc tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down and reframes the original broad "new insights" claim to a more specific and tentative "possible novel indicator," narrowing the scope and reducing certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel combination of normalization methods proves stability results for topological summaries with respect to small changes in the input.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel combination of normalization methods allows for proven stability results for the variables with respect to small changes in the input.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same technical contribution but slightly rephrases it; the meaning (proven stability under small input changes) is clarified and maintained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distribution of centroids is important for differentiating some tissues from their CVT-path counterpart.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The distribution of centroids is important for differentiating some tissues from their CVT-path counterpart.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion is stated the same way in both versions with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of topological summaries may improve the classification accuracy of epithelial images using the Random Forests algorithm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of topological summaries may improve the classification accuracy of epithelial images using a Random Forest algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The claim is substantively the same, but the publication lists this result with higher certainty (publication certainty high vs preprint medium), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discretization greatly improves the robustness of deep learning networks against adversarial attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discretization greatly improves the robustness of deep learning networks against adversarial attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-certainty result verbatim, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Discretizing the input space from 256 values to 4 values extensively improves adversarial robustness for minimal loss in test accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discretizing the input space from 256 values to 4 values extensively improves adversarial robustness with minimal loss in test accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same specific discretization result with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Binary Neural Networks and related variants are intrinsically more robust than their full precision counterparts in adversarial scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Binary neural networks are intrinsically more robust than their full precision counterparts in adversarial scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same assertion about intrinsic robustness of binary networks with unchanged wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining input discretization with Binary Neural Networks further enhances robustness, potentially eliminating the need for adversarial training for certain perturbation magnitudes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining input discretization with binary neural networks waives the need for adversarial training for certain magnitudes of perturbation values. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion about obviating adversarial training for some perturbation sizes but slightly rephrases it; meaning and confidence remain aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Maximal adversarial resistance is observed with 2-bit input discretization, resulting in an adversarial accuracy loss of just ~1-2% compared to clean test accuracy across multiple datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Discretizing the input space from 256 values to 4 values extensively improves adversarial robustness with minimal loss in test accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the 2-bit (4-value) discretization benefit but omits the strong quantitative claim of ~1-2% loss across datasets and additionally introduces later a limitation, so the original stronger quantitative assertion is weakened/narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-free reversible VAMPnets (SRV) is a deep learning architecture that learns nonlinear collective variable approximants to the leading slow eigenfunctions of the transfer operator. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: State-free reversible VAMPnets (SRV) is a deep learning architecture that learns nonlinear collective variable approximants to the leading slow eigenfunctions of the transfer operator. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution statement without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The orthogonality of the learned collective variables is naturally imposed within network training without added regularization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The orthogonality of the learned collective variables is naturally imposed within network training without added regularization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about orthogonality is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned collective variables are explicit and differentiable functions of the input coordinates, making them suitable for enhanced sampling calculations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned collective variables are explicit and differentiable functions of the input coordinates, making them suitable for enhanced sampling calculations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same conclusion about differentiability and suitability for enhanced sampling.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The utility of SRVs is demonstrated in capturing parsimonious nonlinear representations of complex system dynamics in both toy systems and molecular dynamics simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The demonstration claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for obtaining trained activation functions can lead to better results compared to using a pre-defined activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for obtaining a trained activation function can lead to better results than using a pre-defined activation function. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result claim about trained activation functions improving results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method adds local subnetworks with a small number of neurons to the neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method involves adding local sub-networks with a small number of neurons to the neural network. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method does not require learning a large number of additional parameters. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm scales with the number of samples in the 10,000s, significantly improving upon the low 100s of the previous framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm scales with the number of samples n in the 10,000s, significantly improving upon the previous framework which only scaled to the low 100s. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new theory developed in this work models significance and multicollinearity as lazy constraints rather than checking the conditions iteratively. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational results demonstrate that the new algorithm greatly improves accuracy, false detection rate, computational time, and scalability compared to previous algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported computational improvements are restated identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cell segmentation method improves the accuracy of segmentation compared to other state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generalizes well to different datasets without the need to retrain the network for each dataset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Tiny-Net, a lightweight residual structure within R^2-CNN, enables fast and powerful feature extraction from large input images.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Stain variation affects the performance of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained on images from one pathology laboratory when applied to images from another laboratory.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study compares various techniques for reducing generalization error due to stain variation and quantifies their effect on CNN classification performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The BigEarthNet is significantly larger than existing archives in remote sensing, making it more convenient for training deep learning models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A shallow Convolutional Neural Network trained on the BigEarthNet achieves much higher accuracy in remote sensing image scene classification compared to a state-of-the-art CNN model pre-trained on ImageNet.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The BigEarthNet opens up promising directions for advancing operational remote sensing applications and research.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The diurnal cycle of tropical cyclones is characterized by cyclical pulses in the cloud field that propagate radially outward from the center of nearly all Atlantic-basin TCs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A method for quantifying the TC diurnal cycle using one-dimensional persistent homology has been developed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient training algorithm for a subset of classically simulable quantum circuit models is proposed.
