alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical in wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LSTM approach shows great potential by outperforming traditionally utilized time series models in predicting the default risk of borrowers in Lending Club. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LSTM approach shows great potential by outperforming traditionally utilized time series models in predicting the default risk of borrowers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit 'Lending Club' dataset mention, broadening the claim's applicability while keeping the performance assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating the macroeconomic feature unemployment rate can improve the LSTM performance by decreasing RMSE on both the training and testing datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating the macroeconomic feature unemp_rate can improve the LSTM performance by decreasing RMSE on both the training and testing datasets. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The methodological and result content is the same despite minor terminology change ('unemp_rate').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study can guide investors in making investment strategies by broadening the applications of the LSTM algorithm on sequential P2P data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study can broaden the applications of the LSTM algorithm by using it on sequential P2P data and guide investors in making investment strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion content is equivalent; publication rephrases but does not change meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed unsupervised learning algorithm exploits spatially-regularized random walks to cluster hyperspectral image data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed unsupervised learning algorithm effectively clusters hyperspectral image data by leveraging spatially regularized random walks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with nearly identical wording and intent, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incorporation of spatial regularity into the diffusion construction leads to smoother random processes that are more adapted for unsupervised machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of spatial regularity into the diffusion construction leads to smoother random processes that are better suited for unsupervised machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication presents the finding with lower stated certainty (medium vs high), so the claim is substantively unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has low computational complexity and performs competitively against state-of-the-art HSI clustering algorithms on real data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates low computational complexity and competitive performance against state-of-the-art hyperspectral image clustering algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance and complexity claim with equivalent strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed spatial regularization confers an empirical advantage over non-regularized methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spatial regularization provides both theoretical and empirical advantages over nonregularized methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the original empirical-only claim to include theoretical advantages, thereby strengthening and broadening the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a unified framework called asymptotically exact data augmentation (AXDA) that encompasses both well-established and more recent approximate augmented models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article studies a unified framework called asymptotically exact data augmentation (AXDA) that encompasses both well-established and more recent approximate augmented models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution using effectively identical language and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AXDA models can benefit from interesting statistical properties and yield efficient inference algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AXDA models can benefit from interesting statistical properties and yield efficient inference algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The claim appears verbatim in the publication with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quality of the proposed approximation in non-asymptotic settings is assessed with several theoretical results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of the proposed approximation in non-asymptotic settings is assessed with several theoretical results.
alignments.rationale: Theoretical assessment wording is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lightweight feature fusion network (LFFN) significantly reduces network parameters while maximizing single image super-resolution (SISR) results.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The lightweight feature fusion network (LFFN) significantly reduces network parameters while maximizing single image super-resolution (SISR) results.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same performance/efficiency claim with equivalent strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LFFN utilizes spindle blocks and a softmax feature fusion module (SFFM) to effectively explore multi-scale contextual information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LFFN utilizes spindle blocks and a softmax feature fusion module (SFFM) to effectively explore multi-scale contextual information.
alignments.rationale: The technical contribution is the same, but the publication lists the contribution with lower stated certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that LFFN outperforms state-of-the-art methods with similar parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that LFFN outperforms state-of-the-art methods with similar parameters.
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text:
The development of an efficient implementation of t-SNE for a $t$-distribution kernel with an arbitrary degree of freedom $
 u$ can reduce the crowding problem and reveal finer cluster structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of an efficient implementation of t-SNE for a t-distribution kernel with an arbitrary degree of freedom can reduce the crowding problem and reveal finer cluster structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries the same methodological/result claim with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text:
Using theoretical analysis and toy examples, it is shown that $
 u<1$ can further reduce the crowding problem compared to standard t-SNE.
alignments.pub_claim_text:
Using theoretical analysis and toy examples, it is shown that a t-distribution kernel with $$
 u <1$$ can further reduce the crowding problem compared to standard t-SNE.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical finding about u<1 is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Heavier-tailed kernels have a striking effect on large real-life data sets such as MNIST, single-cell RNA-sequencing data, and the HathiTrust library.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of heavier-tailed kernels has a striking effect on large real-life data sets such as MNIST, single-cell RNA-sequencing data, and the HathiTrust library.
alignments.rationale: The empirical observation about heavier-tailed kernels on large datasets remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Domain knowledge confirms that the clusters revealed by modifying the tail heaviness of the t-SNE kernel are meaningful.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Domain knowledge confirms that the clusters revealed by the modified t-SNE kernel are meaningful.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion that domain knowledge validates the discovered clusters is preserved with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Modifying the tail heaviness of the t-SNE kernel can yield additional insight into the cluster structure of the data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Modifying the tail heaviness of the t-SNE kernel can yield additional insight into the cluster structure of the data.
alignments.rationale: The broader insight claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed content-based remote sensing image retrieval method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel content-based remote sensing image retrieval method based on Triplet deep metric learning convolutional neural network significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance claim with additional descriptive wording but no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Triplet deep metric learning convolutional neural network effectively extracts representative features of images in a semantic space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Triplet network with metric learning objective function effectively extracts representative features of images in a semantic space.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same; wording differs slightly but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the learned semantic space, images from the same class are close to each other while those from different classes are far apart.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the constructed semantic space, images from the same class are close to each other while those from different classes are far apart.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the semantic space geometry is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple metric measures such as Euclidean distance can be used directly to compare the similarity of images in the semantic space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple metric measures such as Euclidean distance can be used directly to compare the similarity of images in the semantic space.
alignments.rationale: The claim about using simple metrics in the semantic space is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper proves a relationship between information losses and the expected total variation of the estimated neural model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article proves a relationship between information losses and the product of the expected total variation of the estimated neural model with the information about the feature space contained in the hidden representation of that model.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit dependence on the information contained in the hidden representation (a product term), changing the original relationship rather than leaving it unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expected total variation is bounded as a function of the size of randomly sampled datasets without dependence on model complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The expected total variation is bounded as a function of the size of randomly sampled data sets without additional dependence on model complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same bound claim with equivalent wording and the same qualification about model complexity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The obtained bounds on information losses are less sensitive to input compression and are generally much smaller than existing bounds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bounds on information losses obtained in this article are less sensitive to input compression and are generally much smaller than existing bounds.
alignments.rationale: The published claim repeats the same comparative statement about sensitivity to input compression and relative size of the bounds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The bounds derived in this paper correspond well with experimental findings of information compression in neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The bounds obtained correspond well with experiments, indicating their validity.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the correspondence-to-experiments claim but softens certainty (medium) and slightly reframes it as indicating validity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The article explains recent experimental findings of information compression in neural networks that cannot be explained by previous work using the newly obtained bounds.
alignments.rationale: This is a new, stronger explanatory claim in the publication that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (DCNNs) to reconstruct facial texture and shape from single images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Deep Convolutional Neural Networks (DCNNs) to reconstruct facial texture and shape from single images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach achieves facial texture reconstruction with high-frequency details for the first time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For the first time, the method enables facial texture reconstruction with high-frequency details.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 'first time' high-frequency texture reconstruction claim with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates excellent results in photorealistic and identity preserving 3D face reconstructions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieves photorealistic and identity preserving 3D face reconstructions.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same result about photorealism and identity preservation using slightly streamlined wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: State-of-the-art methods are still not capable of modeling textures in high fidelity.
alignments.rationale: The critical limitation about other methods' incapability is not present in the published claims and appears to have been dropped.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LSD-qPAI computes sO2 directly from pixel-wise initial pressure spectra Sp0.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed learned spectral decoloring method enables blood oxygenation measurements to be inferred from multispectral photoacoustic imaging.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same overall capability (inferring blood oxygenation) but in broader method-level terms rather than the preprint's specific 'Sp0' phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initial results suggest that LSD-qPAI is able to obtain accurate sO2 estimates directly from multispectral photoacoustic measurements in silico.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results suggest that the proposed method is able to obtain blood oxygenation estimates from multispectral photoacoustic measurements in silico, in vitro, and in vivo.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the evidence from in silico only to include in vitro and in vivo, and raises the stated confidence level (medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LSD-qPAI provides plausible estimates of blood oxygenation in vivo.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results suggest that the proposed method is able to obtain blood oxygenation estimates from multispectral photoacoustic measurements in silico, in vitro, and in vivo.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports in vivo results as part of a broader set of experiments; the in vivo plausibility claim is preserved but placed within a larger experimental context.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods for estimating blood oxygenation yield inaccurate results in realistic settings due to the unknown and wavelength-dependent influence of light fluence on the signal.
alignments.rationale: This limitation statement about existing methods appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of an intrinsic driver for pattern formation and self-organization in collective robot systems can lead to the evolution of prediction networks paired with action-selection networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of an intrinsic driver for pattern formation and self-organization in collective robot systems can lead to the evolution of prediction networks paired with action-selection networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim about evolving paired networks from the intrinsic driver implementation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Emergent patterns in collective robotic systems are biased towards easily predictable environments and behaviors, resulting in environments of minimal surprise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Emergent patterns in collective robotic systems are biased towards easily predictable environments and behaviors, resulting in environments of minimal surprise.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the same result about bias toward predictable, minimal-surprise environments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates that different swarm densities and the shape of the environment significantly impact the emergent patterns in self-assembly behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that careful configuration of actions, sensor models, and the environment is necessary to stimulate the emergence of complex behaviors.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the finding as a need for careful configuration of multiple factors (actions, sensors, environment) rather than specifically emphasizing densities and shape, narrowing the explicit factors highlighted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resilience of self-assembly behaviors can be analyzed by causing damages to the assembled pattern and observing the self-organized reassembly of the structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resilience of self-assembly behaviors can be analyzed by causing damages to the assembled pattern and observing the self-organized reassembly of the structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same resilience-analysis method but reports a lower certainty (medium) than the preprint (high), hence a tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach of evolving swarm behaviors for resilient self-assembly has been successfully engineered in simulation.
alignments.rationale: This concrete engineering/result-in-simulation statement appears in the publication but was not explicitly claimed in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel hypercomplex number systems introduced in this paper generalize the Cayley-Dickson algebras and real Clifford algebras.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel hypercomplex number systems introduced in this paper generalize the Cayley-Dickson algebras and real Clifford algebras.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution regarding the generalization of those algebras.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-part associative hypercomplex number systems include real numbers, complex numbers, dual numbers, hyperbolic numbers, quaternions, tessarines, and octonions as particular instances.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not explicitly list these instance inclusions; that enumeration is not present among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stability analysis of several discrete-time hypercomplex-valued Hopfield-type neural networks from the literature is confirmed using the theory presented in this paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stability analysis confirms that several discrete-time hypercomplex-valued Hopfield-type neural networks from the literature are stable.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same stability confirmation for previously reported networks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general class of Hopfield-type neural networks on Cayley-Dickson algebras is introduced and its stability analysis is provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general class of Hopfield-type neural networks on Cayley-Dickson algebras has been introduced and analyzed for stability.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of introducing and analyzing stability for a general class on Cayley-Dickson algebras.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The B-projection functions serve to project the activation potential onto the set of all possible states of a hypercomplex-valued neuron.
alignments.rationale: The B-projection function description is a contribution present in the publication but not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep generative EM model can learn a spectral variability model directly from observed data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep generative EM model can learn a spectral variability model directly from observed data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in wording and meaning, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The encoder and decoder of the generative model are trained using pure pixel information, allowing for an unsupervised formulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The encoder and decoder of the generative model are trained using pure pixel information, allowing for an unsupervised formulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about unsupervised training using pure pixel information is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulations using both synthetic and real data indicate that the proposed strategy can outperform competing state-of-the-art algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simulations using both synthetic and real data indicate that the proposed strategy can outperform competing state-of-the-art algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty, so the claim is unchanged semantically but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predictions by all deep learning models were, on average, better than those of the experts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The predictions by all deep learning models were, on average, better than those of the experts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement is the same in both versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-stage object detector outperformed most of the human pathologists on the majority of tumor cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stage object detector outperformed most of the human pathologists on the majority of tumor cases. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There were considerable differences in position selection between pathologists, which could partially explain the high variance reported for the manual mitotic count. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There were considerable differences in position selection between pathologists, which could partially explain the high variance reported for the manual mitotic count. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about position-selection variability is unchanged between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: To achieve better inter-rater agreement, a computer-based area selection is proposed to support the pathologist in the manual mitotic count. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This proposal does not appear in the preprint and is introduced in the publication as an added contribution to improve inter-rater agreement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method predicts calcium scores in less than 0.3 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method predicts calcium scores in less than 0.3 seconds. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Timing claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The intra-class correlation coefficient between predicted and manual calcium scores was 0.98 for both cardiac and chest CT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The intra-class correlation coefficient between predicted and manual calcium scores was 0.98 for both cardiac and chest CT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported ICC values are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method showed almost perfect agreement between automatic and manual CVD risk categorization, with a Cohen's kappa of 0.95 in cardiac CT and 0.93 in chest CT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method showed almost perfect agreement between automatic and manual CVD risk categorization, with a linearly weighted Cohen's kappa of 0.95 in cardiac CT and 0.93 in chest CT. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the reported Cohen's kappa is linearly weighted, which clarifies the metric without changing the numerical results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is hundreds of times faster than state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is hundreds of times faster than state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speedup claim appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Tikhonov regularization approach is effective for reconstructing the estimator of a quantity in non-linear ill-posed inverse problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Tikhonov regularization approach is effective for estimating quantities in non-linear ill-posed inverse problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the preprint for clarity (estimating quantities rather than reconstructing an estimator) without changing the substantive conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The estimator is defined as the minimizer of a Tikhonov functional, which combines a data misfit term and a quadratic penalty term. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimator defined as the minimizer of a Tikhonov functional combines a data misfit term and a quadratic penalty term. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The description of the estimator and the Tikhonov functional is essentially identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical analysis developed for the minimizer of the Tikhonov regularization scheme utilizes the ansatz of reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the RKHS ansatz from the preprint; this specific methodological claim appears to be omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optimal rates of convergence for the proposed scheme are discussed, uniformly over classes of admissible solutions defined through appropriate source conditions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optimal rates of convergence for the proposed Tikhonov regularization scheme are discussed, uniformly over classes of admissible solutions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both versions report discussion of optimal convergence rates uniformly over admissible solution classes; the publication wording is slightly streamlined but the claim remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model combining Deep Adaptive Clustering (DAC) with Spatial Transformer Networks (STN) outperformed the baseline model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model combining Deep Adaptive Clustering (DAC) with Spatial Transformer Networks (STN) outperformed the baseline model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is identical between the preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual attention techniques can reduce the problems classical deep learning methods face with spatial image transformations like scale and rotation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visual attention techniques can reduce the problems classical deep learning methods face with spatial image transformations like scale and rotation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about visual attention techniques is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed model was evaluated using the MNIST and FashionMNIST datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed model was evaluated using the MNIST and FashionMNIST datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation datasets and contribution statement are the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces verification-preserving model updates as the first approach toward obtaining formal safety guarantees for reinforcement learning in complex heterogeneous environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper introduces verification-preserving model updates, the first approach toward obtaining formal safety guarantees for reinforcement learning in settings with multiple possible environmental models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution but rephrases "complex heterogeneous environments" as "settings with multiple possible environmental models," which clarifies the intended scenario without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing formal methods for learning and optimization primarily focus on constrained learning or constrained optimization with a single correct model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing formal methods for learning and optimization primarily consider constrained learning or constrained optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the preprint's specific claim about a "single correct model," removing that restriction and thereby broadening the scope of the statement; this is a substantive revision rather than mere wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach combines design-time model updates and runtime model falsification to provide formal safety proofs for autonomous systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach combines inductive data and deductive proving with design-time model updates and runtime model falsification.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail (``inductive data and deductive proving'') while preserving the original claim that design-time updates and runtime falsification are combined to enable formal proofs; this clarifies the method without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising reconstructions of larger spatial extents of existing white matter bundles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising reconstructions of larger spatial extent of existing white matter bundles.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is the same as the preprint claim with only minor wording differences; meaning and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising reconstructions with fewer false positives.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising reconstructions of less false positives.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint claim (wording slightly altered) about reduced false positives; no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising robustness to known position and shape biases of current tractography techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ML-based techniques have shown promising robustness to known position and shape biases of current tractography techniques.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim repeated in the publication; no meaningful change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: None of the ML-based methods have shown conclusive performances or have been adopted as a de facto solution to tractography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: None of the ML-based methods have shown conclusive performances or have been adopted as a de facto solution to tractography.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lack of well-defined and extensive frameworks to train, evaluate, and compare these methods may hinder their adoption.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a lack of well-defined and extensive frameworks to train, evaluate, and compare ML methods in tractography.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the lack of frameworks but omits the preprint's conjecture that this "may hinder adoption," making the statement more direct; the core observation remains but is phrased differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method for covariance and precision matrix estimation largely outperforms the sample covariance matrix estimate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for covariance and precision matrix estimation largely outperforms the sample covariance matrix estimate.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's performance claim directly; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method competes with state-of-the-art methods while being computationally simpler.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method competes with state-of-the-art methods while being computationally simpler and faster.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that the method is "faster" in addition to "simpler," strengthening the performance claim (amplified) while retaining the same scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applications to linear and quadratic discriminant analyses demonstrate significant gains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applications to linear and quadratic discriminant analyses show significant gains from the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same application claim but lowers reported certainty (high -> medium), so the claim is clarified with reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings suggest practical interest to statistical machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method suggests practical relevance for statistical machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same conclusion but with weaker stated certainty (publication labels conclusion as low certainty), so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel semantic framework for modeling linguistic expressions of generalization is presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel semantic framework for modeling linguistic expressions of generalization is presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint contribution verbatim; no change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A dataset covering the entirety of the Universal Dependencies English Web Treebank is constructed using the proposed framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A dataset covering the entirety of the Universal Dependencies English Web Treebank is constructed using the proposed framework.
alignments.rationale: The dataset construction claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of type-level and token-level information for predicting expressions of generalization is probed using the constructed dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset is used to probe the efficacy of type-level and token-level information for predicting expressions of generalization.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the experimental claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A general method based on spectral analysis of the transition matrix of the continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC) can be constructed without the need for a population structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A general method based on spectral analysis of the transition matrix of the continuous-time Markov chain (CTMC) can be constructed without the need for a population structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological claim verbatim; no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes diffusion maps to analyze the transition matrix as the operator of a hidden continuous process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes diffusion maps to analyze the transition matrix as the operator of a hidden continuous process.

alignments.pub_claim_text: In the real-time risk prediction setting, the model could proactively intervene with 21% more patients and before 76% more missed doses than current heuristic baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The average approximation error in practice is much smaller than the approximation guarantee $$\frac{\eta}{2}$$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same empirical observation about approximation error being much smaller than the theoretical guarantee, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves significant speed-ups with only a modest sacrifice in accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed scheme achieves significant speed-ups with only a modest sacrifice in accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the trade-off claim (large speed gains for modest accuracy loss) with the same framing and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Uncertainty quantification and active learning can be achieved by Bayesian semi-supervised graph convolutional neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Bayesian semi-supervised graph convolutional neural networks can achieve uncertainty quantification and active learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication directly restates the same high-level result regarding Bayesian semi-supervised GCNs for uncertainty and active learning, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian approach estimates uncertainty in a statistically principled way through sampling from the posterior distribution. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian approach estimates uncertainty in a statistically principled way through sampling from the posterior distribution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about posterior sampling providing principled uncertainty estimates; substance and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Semi-supervised learning disentangles representation learning and regression, keeping uncertainty estimates accurate in the low data limit. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Semi-supervised learning disentangles representation learning and regression, maintaining accurate uncertainty estimates in low data conditions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same idea about disentangling and maintaining accuracy of uncertainty estimates under low-data conditions; wording is slightly adjusted but meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models need to reliably predict when they will fail, even when the training data is biased. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models need to reliably predict when they will fail, even with biased training data, to replace costly experiments. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core need-for-failure-prediction claim but adds an application motivation (replacing costly experiments), clarifying the intended use-case.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study highlights the promise of Bayesian deep learning for chemistry. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This concluding claim about promise for chemistry does not appear in the preprint claim set and is a new, added publication-level takeaway.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal experiment design (OED) methodology optimizes the geometry of the parametric joint-confidence regions (CRs) using available experimental degrees of freedom. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal experiment design (OED) methodology optimizes the geometry of the parametric joint-confidence regions (CRs) using available experimental degrees of freedom. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core methodological contribution about optimizing the geometry of joint-confidence regions, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel approximation technique for exact CRs using inner- and outer-approximating ellipsoids as a computationally less demanding alternative. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a novel approximation technique for exact CRs using inner- and outer-approximating ellipsoids as a computationally less demanding alternative. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution description regarding the ellipsoidal inner/outer approximations as a computationally cheaper alternative.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed OED framework explicitly considers the exact CRs, unlike commonly used approaches that rely on linearized CRs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The comparison of optimal designs from the proposed method with those from commonly used linearization-based approaches reveals differences in performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the explicit-CRs-versus-linearization claim verbatim but reports a comparison showing performance differences; this softens and reframes the original claim, so it is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of two simple heuristic numerical schemes for bilevel optimization is assessed within the context of the studied OED problems. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to assessing two specific heuristic numerical schemes; that explicit evaluation claim is not present in the listed publication claims and appears to have been removed.
